text: From the Division of Interventional Pulmonology, Yashoda Hospitals, Hyderabad, today we present a case of complex tracheal stenosis following tracheostomy and bronchoscopic management.
This 65-year-old patient was admitted at a peripheral center with severe septic shock requiring mechanical ventilatory support, following which he underwent a tracheostomy.
Following discharge, on his review consultation, a diagnostic bronchoscopy was performed which showed complex tracheal stenosis with the tracheal cartilages fractured and protruding into the lumen.
Rigid bronchoscopy guided clearance of the broken cartilage pieces were extracted and mechanical dilatation using the tracheoscope was performed.
After achieving a significant lumen, a silicon stent was placed, covering the defect successfully, restoring the luminal patency of the trachea.
This case video explains the complications of tracheostomy with fracture of the tracheal cartilages during the tracheostomy procedure.
This video explains the management of complex tracheal stenosis and silicon stent placement in short.
